description: Default-deny check: `planner provider-probe` invoked without PLANNER_PROBE=1 (env unset / "0" / "true" / "" / typos) MUST exit 2 with one-line stderr policy refusal and ZERO network calls. Verified structurally: the CLI gate is enforced by `_probe_gate_open()` BEFORE provider resolution / settings build / model-config load, so the probe never reaches `urllib.request.urlopen`. The replay asserts exit code 2 + one-line stderr policy refusal markers; the no-network guarantee is by construction (gate runs first), not by a runtime monkeypatch. Future hardening could add an in-process monkeypatch layer (patch urllib.request.urlopen to raise AssertionError and assert the patch never fires) — tracked separately.
assertions: PLANNER_PROBE unset OR != "1" → exit 2 with one-line stderr policy refusal

no urllib.request.urlopen call is reachable: the gate check runs BEFORE provider resolution and settings build, so even an invalid --provider does not trigger any network layer (asserted structurally, not by runtime monkeypatch)
stderr contains 'opt-in only' and 'PLANNER_PROBE=1' as substring markers
stdout is empty (policy refusal is on stderr)
exact-match gate: PLANNER_PROBE='0' / 'true' / 'yes' / '1.0' / ' 1' all keep gate closed (strict literal "1" only)
the gate check happens BEFORE provider resolution or settings build — even an invalid --provider does not trigger network
